title: feat(dedup): prioritize disputed findings in semantic dedup candidate selection
body:
## Description

Currently `FindCandidates()` fills the MaxCandidates budget in encounter order. If disputed/acknowledged findings are encountered late, they may overflow and bypass semantic comparison. This means previously-disputed findings could be re-posted if the LLM rephrases them.

## Proposed Solution

- Two-pass candidate selection in `FindCandidates()`
- Pass 1: Collect candidates where existing finding is disputed/acknowledged (priority)
- Pass 2: Fill remaining budget with open finding candidates
- This ensures disputed findings always get semantic comparison even at scale

## Files to Modify

- `internal/usecase/dedup/candidates.go` - Modify `FindCandidates()` signature and logic
- `internal/usecase/github/poster.go` - Pass `existingStatuses` to `FindCandidates()`
- `internal/usecase/dedup/candidates_test.go` - Add priority ordering tests

## Context

Part of defense-in-depth against review noise at scale. See [platform#78](https://github.com/delightfulhammers/platform/pull/78) analysis.

When a PR has 88+ findings (like platform#78), the 200-candidate budget (raised from 50) may still overflow. By prioritizing disputed findings, we ensure that acknowledged/disputed findings always get semantic comparison even when budget is tight.

## Test Cases

1. Disputed findings fill budget first
2. Open findings fill remaining budget  
3. Overflow only contains open findings when budget exceeded
4. Mix of disputed/open handled correctly
